channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bin overflowing, 6th April
item.description:
Bin overflowing
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marlow Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped mattress. Still there (wrongly reported as fixed on your website)., 6th April
item.description:
Dumped mattress. Incorrectly listed on your website as 'fixed'. It is definitely still there, so the report on 25th March was seemingly wrongly dealt with. Location = What3Words: plates.script.stacks (West side of Brampton Road, opposite cut-through from Burcharbro Road).

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burcharbro Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent – Fly-tipping, Health Hazard & Vermin at Fortune Walk (SE28 0EL), 6th April
item.description:
There is a significant amount of fly-tipped rubbish on the public footpath at the end of Fortune Walk, at the junction with Garrick Drive (SE28 0EL). In addition, there are two trolley bins (one without a lid and one with the wrong lid) that have been left uncollected for several months. These bins appear abandoned, and the waste is now overflowing and spreading across the footpath. This situation has become a serious health and safety risk, with rubbish scattered in the area and rats already sighted. This requires urgent investigation and immediate clearance

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chamberlain Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Every day people fly tip all sorts of rubbish in this alley

item.title: Abandoned temporary road sign, 6th April
item.description:
A “no parking” temporary sign has been abandoned on the pavement outside Clifford’s Inn, Fetter Lane. It’s potentially blocking the pavement..

item.title: 91 Brondesbury villas, 6th April
item.description:
Road need sweeping before the rubbish goes into the nearest drain and cause the road to flood

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree being flat 3 marlston munster square, 6th April
item.description:
I’m writing to ask for some help regarding the trees at the back of my property. Over time they have become more of a concern for us, especially because branches often fall from them. As a family with four children who love being outdoors and spending time in the garden, this is something we are finding increasingly worrying. It does not feel particularly safe, and I am concerned that someone could end up getting hurt if it is not looked at. The trees are also now overhanging the property, which seems to be getting worse. Recently, tree surgeons were working at number 7 removing a tree in the back garden, and during that visit it was mentioned that there is some dead wood in one of the trees in question that would need attention. Given the falling branches, that added to my concern. I have also recently learned that these are London plane trees, and I understand they can cause respiratory and eye irritation because of the fine hairs they release. This may explain why my children seem to have extra coughing and irritation during the spring and summer months. I have noticed that some trees in the surrounding area have already been cut back much more heavily, to the point where there are very few leaves or small branches growing back and am wondering if this is for the same reasons as my own . For example: - Munster Square, outside the building called Swallowfield – 1 tree - Munster Square, in front of the NCP car park – 1 tree - Prince of Wales Passage – 4 trees I am just hoping the council can inspect these trees and see whether they need maintenance or cutting back for safety reasons. I would really appreciate your help with this. Many thanks in advanced
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Cambridge Terrace Mews, Fitzrovia, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 abandoned bikes outside #57/#59, 6th April
item.description:
3 abandoned bikes outside #57/59. One has missing seat and tyres.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Prince George Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All road markings gone, 6th April
item.description:
As you come off the a2 at the woolwich flyover all the road markings are gone.there is nothing showing all the individual lanes and give way lines.this is all over the whole of the underneath of the flyover.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackwall Tunnel Approach, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road markings, 6th April
item.description:
As you come off the a2 leading to the roundabout at danson road,the small one way street has been resurfaced but the road markings have not been redone.the road should be a 2 lane road with traffic going to the left in what should be the left lane and traffic going straight on or right in the right lane,but it has been left like it is just a single lane causing a build up of traffic
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Underpass, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
